text: I take in my surroundings. Where am I, and what's nearby?
I rise, gather my satchel and longbow, and head down the creaking stairs to see who's in the common room.
I approach the bearded innkeeper and ask if he can spare a bowl of something hot. I drop two silver pieces on the bar.
I lean in, lowering my voice to match his. 'What is it?' I keep my eyes on him, but my hand drifts near the satchel.
'Before what?' I ask quietly. I'm aware of the two farmers at the window table — I glance at them once, then back to him.
I take my bowl, walk over to the farmer who lost his goats, and sit across from him. 'Mind if I share your table? I'd like to hear the story straight from the man it happened to.'
I nod respectfully to the younger one. 'Kael Windrunner. The innkeeper said you found your goats with their ribs showing. What else have you seen?'
'No blood?' I lean in. 'Anyone else lost livestock the same way? Anyone seen anything in the woods at night?'
I turn toward the new voice. 'Who said that?'
I focus on the cook. 'Your boy went where, Marta? Tell me everything.'
I rise from the table. 'I need air. I'll be back.' I step outside the inn into the morning road and look up at the misty foothills.
I walk along the road, scanning the tree line for tracks or any sign of movement. Where do the woods grow thickest?
I crouch by the gap in the fence and study the ground. Any prints? Disturbed brambles? I want to know what's been passing through.
I rise and head back toward the inn. I want to talk to the older farmer again — Bram knows this land better than anyone.
I sit back down at the window table next to old Bram. 'I found a gap in the fence south of here. Game trail leads in. Have you been past it lately?'
'When you searched for Finn?' I keep my voice low so the cook by the hearth doesn't have to relive it. 'How far in did you get?'
I turn to the lean youngster. 'What did you spot from up there? Anything at all that didn't fit?'
'Where what?' I prompt the young farmer when he trails off.
I push back from the table. 'I'd like to see this dark patch myself. Old Bram, would you guide me to the ridge? Or you, Reed?'
I nod to the older man. To Marta at the hearth, I add: 'We'll be back before noon. If anyone asks for me, tell the gruff barkeep where I went.'
I follow the weathered farmer down the road. As we walk, I ask, 'Has the cook lost any other family beside Finn?'
We follow the trail into the brambles. I keep a hand near my bow. After a few minutes I ask the old goat-keeper, 'How long until we reach the ridge?'
